page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1ne-c8d3.8xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware characteristic is its massive bundled local storage capacity of 70,866 GB delivered via HDD. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1108.59 and a poor single-threaded gzip compression rate of 19.07 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core execution is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 23,516.82 and a PassMark CPU score of 22,938.9. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth reaches 1,309.89 GB/sec, the overall PassMark memory score is weak at 1,821.44.

Given the massive 70,866 GB HDD storage capacity bundled with 32 dedicated cores, this instance offers high resource density for storage-heavy workloads, presenting a cost-efficient profile for massive data retention without requiring external block storage attachments. However, the reliance on HDD storage and weak single-core CPU performance represent clear tradeoffs, making it less suitable for latency-sensitive or compute-intensive applications. It is best suited for large-scale data warehousing, cold storage, backup repositories, and multi-threaded batch processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1ne-c8d3.8xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a massive bundled local storage capacity of 70,866 GB HDD. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is designed for storage-intensive workloads. Benchmark results indicate weak single-core performance, such as a stress-ng single-core score of 1108.59, but average multi-core capabilities with a PassMark CPU score of 22,938.9. Memory performance is also weak, as shown by its PassMark memory score of 1,821.44. This hardware profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for massive data storage and multi-threaded batch processing, though it is less suitable for latency-sensitive or high-compute applications.

tagline: A storage-dense server featuring massive bundled HDD capacity and dedicated multi-core processing for large-scale data archiving and batch workloads.
